fields.name: Standard Sales Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the standard sales code that this line is defined for.
When you set up the standard sales code, the program copies the standard sales code from the Code field in the Standard Sales Code table.

fields.name: Line No.
fields.tooltip:
Shows the line number.
The program numbers the lines sequentially and uses the numbers to keep track of the different standard sales lines associated with each code.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the line is for a general ledger account, item, resource, fixed asset or item charge.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of a general ledger account, item, resource, additional cost, or fixed asset, depending on the contents of the Type field.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the entry, which is based on the contents of the Type and No. fields.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of units of the item on the line.

fields.name: Amount Excl. VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net amount for the standard sales line. This field only applies to lines of type G/L Account and Charge (Item).

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.
